svg.title: InfiniBand GRH (Global Route Header) field layout
svg.desc: Bit-level breakdown of the 40-byte IB GRH across five 32-bit words
svg.text: GRH — Global Route Header (40 bytes / 320 bits)
Present only when LNH = 11; structure is fully aligned with the IPv6 header
Always 6 — aligned with the IPv6 version field
Traffic Class — used for QoS marking, equivalent to IPv6 Traffic Class
Identifies a flow associated with a specific QP connection
Payload length in bytes following the GRH; does not include the GRH itself
Next header type: 0x1B = BTH (IB Transport Header)
Hop limit — decremented by each IB router; packet is discarded when it reaches zero
128-bit global addresses in IPv6 format; in native IB: 64-bit subnet prefix + 64-bit GUID
